cards.back: Natural Language Processing (NLP) is a subfield of artificial intelligence that deals with the interaction between computers and humans in natural language.
The origins of NLP date back to the 1950s with the Dartmouth Summer Research Project on Artificial Intelligence, where the term NLP was first coined.
Some of the major challenges of NLP include handling ambiguity, sarcasm, idioms, and nuances of language, as well as dealing with noise and variability in language data.
Processing Indian languages such as Hindi, Tamil, and Telugu poses unique challenges due to their complex grammar and script, as well as limited availability of training data.
Some common applications of NLP include text classification, sentiment analysis, machine translation, and speech recognition, with a growing focus on applications in healthcare and customer service.
